headline: In Lebanon, a fragile cease-fire between Israel and Hezbollah begins
description: The Lebanese army accused Israel of repeated truce violations, while Hezbollah said it retaliated against Israeli troops. Talks between the two sides mark a shift, but core disputes remain, including the withdrawal of the Israeli army and Hezbollah's disarmament.

headline: How the tech lobby made secrecy part of EU law on data centers
Andrew Puzder, a Trump loyalist amid European diplomats
Why the Hormuz 'mission' led by France and the UK is largely symbolic
description: Wanting to avoid being drawn into the US's blockade of the key shipping route, Macron and Starmer aim to establish a multilateral and 'strictly defensive' plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
description: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.

headline: 'Romeo and Juliet,' choreographed by Rudolf Nureyev, returns to Paris
description: The spectacular production at the Opéra Bastille gives the new generation of dancers a chance to showcase their talents.

headline: Suspense and pretense: The RN prepares for life after Le Pen
description: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.

'After Greenland and Iran, the Hungarian election marks a new step in the transatlantic rift'
description: The crushing electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, despite massive support from Washington, underscores the failure of Donald Trump's ideological campaign in Europe. The gap is widening inexorably, writes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

headline: Virginie Despentes and over 130 writers quit publishing house after Bolloré fires CEO
description: More than 130 writers said they would be leaving the Hachette Livre group after billionaire Vincent Bolloré fired Olivier Nora, the publisher of the Grasset house.

headline: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
description: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.
